tasks.title: Xcode project scaffolding: targets, App Group, StoreKit config, git init, synchronized folder groups
acceptance_criteria: Kitbag.xcodeproj opens and builds an empty shell via xcodebuild
main app target, KitbagWidget extension target, KitbagTests (Swift Testing) and KitbagUITests (XCUITest) targets all exist and compile
App Group container is provably readable and writable from both the main app target and the widget extension target via a throwaway smoke test committed to the repo
Kitbag.storekit contains real product identifiers for monthly/annual premium
git repository initialized with lane branches cut from main after this task lands

Sources/DataDomain, Sources/ServicesUtilities, Sources/PrimaryUI, Sources/PolishResilience folders exist as synchronized groups mapped to correct target membership so later file adds never require pbxproj edits

tasks.title: Three-tab navigation shell and design tokens
acceptance_criteria: App launches into Trips/Insights/Settings tabs each with an independent NavigationStack
a real kill-and-relaunch resets every tab to its root, no persisted deep-stack restoration
spacing/corner-radius/color-story token values match the locked design_handoff values

tasks.title: SwiftData models and VersionedSchema
acceptance_criteria: ModelContainer builds successfully from SchemaV1
a unit test creates a Trip with items and a review and confirms cascade-delete removes both when the trip is deleted
didHappen defaults to true and is the only persisted lifecycle fact on Trip

tasks.title: Schema migration mechanism regression test
acceptance_criteria: a standalone test-only two-version schema pair (not SchemaV1) proves ModelContainer migration works on this SDK/Xcode version
test is kept permanently as a regression guard, not deleted after passing once

tasks.title: Bundled climate dataset and ClimateCatalog
acceptance_criteria: 30-40 bundled regions plus fallback climate zones each have all 12 months present
a plausibility-bounds test rejects unrealistic temperature/precipitation values, not just checking coverage
ClimateCatalog loads once in-memory at launch with no per-lookup file IO

tasks.title: Pure derivation functions: tripPhase, monthOfMajorityDays, isWithinFreeRetentionWindow
acceptance_criteria: month-boundary tie-break rule is deterministic and unit-tested with a trip that spans exactly two months
isWithinFreeRetentionWindow is relationship-free (takes only ids/dates/counts, never a SwiftData relationship traversal) and unit-tested at the exact 3rd/4th trip boundary
tripPhase is never persisted and is recomputed correctly for every TripPhase case including didNotHappen

tasks.title: Pure GenerationEngine and the learning go/no-go test
acceptance_criteria: generate(input:) is synchronous, non-async, non-actor-isolated
reviewing trip A with an item marked unused, then generating a same-type-or-region trip B, produces that item visibly demoted with a reason string naming trip A
a single data point never shifts a suggestion; the minimumEvidenceCount=2 rule is explicitly tested
zero-history input still produces a non-empty, sensible list from trip type + climate alone

tasks.title: applyGenerationResult and SwiftData-backed LearningHistoryReader
acceptance_criteria: applying a regenerated result preserves isCustom rows and preserves checked state on unchanged suggested rows
isRecentlyChanged is set only on rows whose reason actually changed from the prior frozen snapshot
LearningHistoryReader recomputes fresh from raw ReviewItemOutcome rows every call, never an incremental cache, and only reads trips where didHappen == true

tasks.title: Trip/GeneratedList/Review repositories
acceptance_criteria: checked state, custom items, and a saved review all survive a real kill-and-relaunch in an integration test
deleting a trip cascade-deletes items and review; markTripDidNotHappen only flips didHappen and never deletes
toggleChecked performs a single targeted field write, verified by asserting only one row changes, not a full-list re-save
saveReview is full-overwrite so re-editing a review is deterministic, never incremental patching

tasks.title: PackingCoordinator: the only bridge between SwiftData and the pure engine
acceptance_criteria: createTripAndGenerate, regenerateList, submitReview, and rebuildAllDerivedState are all integration-tested against real repositories
generation always runs synchronously on the main actor with no real suspension point; a test asserts no spinner is needed on the golden path
rebuildAllDerivedState correctly repairs a deliberately corrupted derived-state fixture in a test

tasks.title: Trip creation and edit flow
acceptance_criteria: a brand-new user with zero history creates a trip fully offline and gets a non-empty, reason-stringed list in the same session
destination search shows the climate-zone fallback the instant results are empty, with no debounce/async/loading state
editing a trip's dates/destination regenerates suggested items but preserves custom items and checked state, showing the RegenerationBanner only when the diff actually affects generation

tasks.title: Trip list and trip detail/checklist screens
acceptance_criteria: empty/loading/success/error states all present on both screens, plus a distinct 'generating' state on trip detail separate from ordinary loading
checked state and custom items survive a real kill-and-relaunch
every suggested item's reason sheet shows a real, specific reason string, never generic 'smart suggestion' copy
locked (beyond-free-tier) trip cards render instantly using isWithinFreeRetentionWindow with no fetch into items/review

tasks.title: Post-trip review flow including didn't-happen exit
acceptance_criteria: a zero-exception review completes in 3 taps or fewer, measured by an XCUITest, not eyeballed
the 'this trip didn't happen' exit is visually and semantically distinct from a normal review and requires its own confirmation
a completed review is reopenable and editable, and downstream aggregates recompute deterministically from full history, not incrementally

tasks.title: Changed-item badge and first-time toast for the review-driven regeneration moment
acceptance_criteria: after a review-driven regeneration, affected items show a persistent inline badge
exactly one banner/toast ever fires for this moment across the app's lifetime, gated by a single permanent flag that never resets
the badge clears when its item is checked off

tasks.title: EntitlementStore and StoreKit 2 wiring
acceptance_criteria: a long-lived Transaction.updates listener drives entitlement state, never just a launch-time check
a simulated purchase and a simulated expiration/renewal-failure are both exercised against the local .storekit config
an offline grace window biases toward preserving already-unlocked access rather than flapping locked/unlocked
trip creation and the core packing flow never block on entitlement checks

tasks.title: Paywall and Settings screens
acceptance_criteria: paywall reflects real transaction state and includes a working restore-purchases action
settings states on-device privacy as a stated feature, shows climate data provenance copy, and gates export/share behind the real entitlement
every destructive action (delete trip, clear history) requires confirmation before proceeding

tasks.title: Insights screen
acceptance_criteria: an honest empty state shows before any review exists, with no fabricated sample data
populated state shows only real, attributable behavior patterns from review history
beyond the free-tier window, a blurred-but-visible teaser is shown rather than an empty lock screen

tasks.title: Export/share with reasoning stripped
acceptance_criteria: exported plain-text list contains item names/quantities only, with all reason/analytic commentary stripped, verified by a test asserting the reason strings never appear in export output

tasks.title: AppError and PersistenceBootstrap full-bleed error handling
acceptance_criteria: a simulated ModelContainer init failure renders a full-bleed error state in a test/preview, never a crash
every AppError case maps to a reachable, distinct UI error state somewhere in the app

tasks.title: Accessibility header collapse adapter
acceptance_criteria: all four legitimate combinations (full+gradient, full+flat, collapsed+gradient, collapsed+flat) are unit-tested since Dynamic-Type-XXL layout collapse and Increase-Contrast/Reduce-Transparency color flattening are independent and can both be active at once
ColorStoryHeaderView visibly degrades correctly under Dynamic Type XXL and under Increase Contrast/Reduce Transparency

tasks.title: WidgetSnapshot, publisher, and KitbagWidget extension
acceptance_criteria: WidgetSnapshot structurally never contains destination text, verified by a test inspecting its Codable field set
publish is debounced over roughly 1-2 seconds of inactivity before calling WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines
timeline provider output is unit-tested; on-device rendering is explicitly flagged unverifiable in this headless environment via BuildVerificationChecklist, never claimed as working

tasks.title: Live Activity for an active packing session
acceptance_criteria: start/update/end logic is unit-tested against the ActivityKit attributes/content-state shape
on-device rendering is flagged unverifiable in this headless environment, not asserted as working
this is the first feature cut if schedule is squeezed, per the standing cut order

tasks.title: AddPackingItemIntent and NotificationScheduler
acceptance_criteria: the intent targets the most imminent upcoming trip and has a clean spoken failure path at zero trips, unit-tested
a test proves the core packing flow works identically whether notification permission is granted or denied
notification copy is generic and never contains destination text

tasks.title: DeepLinkRouter and resolution logic
acceptance_criteria: resolve() returns .tripNotFound for a deleted or didn't-happen trip id instead of crashing or silently no-opping
a single resolution point prevents simultaneous notification/widget/Siri triggers from racing each other

tasks.title: Wire deep-link intents and toast fallback into the app root
acceptance_criteria: tapping a stale notification/widget/Siri deep link lands safely on Trip List with the shared toast reading that the trip is no longer available
AppRootView.swift (owned by T-002) is only modified here to add intent resolution, not restructured

tasks.title: Accessibility and empty/loading/error-state audit
acceptance_criteria: VoiceOver reads item name, checked state, suggested-vs-custom, and reason/changed status in a sensible order on every screen that has them
no screen distinguishes suggested/custom/locked/review-exception by color alone
delete-trip and mark-didn't-happen confirmations have distinctly different VoiceOver labels and copy weight

tasks.title: Checklist interaction performance verification
acceptance_criteria: toggling a single item in a 100+ item checklist is measured to trigger exactly one targeted write, not a full-list re-fetch or re-save

tasks.title: Final BuildVerificationChecklist sweep
acceptance_criteria: verifiedCoreFlows lists every core workflow that actually has a passing automated test, by name
unverifiableInHeadless lists widget/Live Activity/Siri on-device rendering and live StoreKit purchases explicitly, never marked as working
knownLimitations is populated from whatever this sweep actually finds, not left empty by default
